apiBoundary: external calls go through a typed client/service layer — no inline httpx/requests in routers

researchLayers.name: routers
researchLayers.targets: APIRouter modules, path operations, dependencies (Depends), status codes

researchLayers.name: services
researchLayers.targets: business logic modules, the client/service boundary, background tasks

researchLayers.name: data
researchLayers.targets: SQLAlchemy/SQLModel models, repositories, sessions, Alembic migrations

researchLayers.name: build
researchLayers.targets: pyproject.toml, uv.lock, ASGI server config, CI

_notes: Starter profile for a FastAPI service managed with uv. Assumes a pyproject.toml + uv.lock, an 'app/' package exposing app.main:app, and a pytest 'integration' marker. Copy to working-docs/config.json and adjust.

uv is the default: 'uv sync' installs from the lockfile and 'uv run <tool>' runs inside the project venv (no manual activation). pip alternative → INSTALL 'pip install -r requirements.txt', drop the 'uv run' prefixes. Poetry → INSTALL 'poetry install', prefix verbs with 'poetry run'.
Lint/format use Ruff (ruff check + ruff format). Swap for flake8/black/isort if that's your toolchain.
TYPECHECK uses mypy; if you don't type-check, set it to 'MANUAL:' or remove it from the mappings.
INTEGRATION_TEST uses a pytest marker (@pytest.mark.integration) so slow tests (DB/Testcontainers, TestClient end-to-end) run separately from unit tests — adjust to your convention.
apiBoundary encodes 'outbound I/O lives behind typed clients, not scattered through routers' — adjust to match your repo's CLAUDE.md.
Set jira.mode to 'mcp' if the Atlassian MCP connector is available in your Claude Code session.
